channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Android gets patches for Qualcomm zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Google has released security updates to patch 129 Android security vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in a Qualcomm display component. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The act...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
item.description: Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet For...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 30 Alleged Members of 'The Com' Arrested in Project Compass
item.description: The global law enforcement crackdown, which began in January 2025, also identified nearly 180 members of the notorious cybercriminal collective....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Google Security site uses PWA app to steal credentials, MFA codes
item.description: A phishing campaign is using a fake Google Account security page to deliver a web-based app capable of stealing one-time passcodes, harvesting cryptocurrency wallet addresses, and proxying attacker tr...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Alabama man pleads guilty to hacking, extorting hundreds of women
item.description: A 22-year-old Alabama man pleaded guilty to extortion, cyberstalking, and computer fraud charges after hijacking the social media accounts of hundreds of young women (including minors). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Florida woman imprisoned for massive Microsoft license fraud scheme
item.description: A Florida woman was sentenced to 22 months in prison for running a massive years-long scheme to traffic thousands of stolen Microsoft Certificate of Authenticity (COA) labels. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Develops Merkle Tree Certificates to Enable Quantum-Resistant HTTPS in Chrome
item.description: Google has announced a new program in its Chrome browser to ensure that HTTPS certificates are secure against the future risk posed by quantum computers. "To ensure the scalability and efficiency of t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: UK warns of Iranian cyberattack risks amid Middle-East conflict
item.description: The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) alerted British organizations to a heightened risk of Iranian cyberattacks amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How Deepfakes and Injection Attacks Are Breaking Identity Verification
item.description: Deepfakes and injection attacks are targeting identity verification moments, from onboarding to account recovery. Incode explains why enterprises must validate the full session—media, device integrity...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access control, e...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic confirms Claude is down in a worldwide outage
item.description: Claude appears to be having a major outage right now, with elevated errors reported across all platforms. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How to Protect Your SaaS from Bot Attacks with SafeLine WAF
item.description: Most SaaS teams remember the day their user traffic started growing fast. Few notice the day bots started targeting them. On paper, everything looks great: more sign-ups, more sessions, more API calls...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The vulnerabili...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bug in Google's Gemini AI Panel Opens Door to Hijacking
item.description: Attackers could have exploited the vulnerability to escalate privileges, violate user privacy while browsing, and access sensitive resources....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm reg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
item.description: Security researchers have disclosed a high-severity vulnerability dubbed "ClawJacked" in the popular AI agent OpenClaw that allowed a malicious website to silently bruteforce access to a locally runni...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Samsung TVs to stop collecting Texans’ data without express consent
item.description: Samsung and the State of Texas have reached a settlement agreement over the alleged unlawful collection of content-viewing information through its smart TVs [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: QuickLens Chrome extension steals crypto, shows ClickFix attack
item.description: A Chrome extension named "QuickLens - Search Screen with Google Lens" has been removed from the Chrome Web Store after it was compromised to push malware and attempt to steal crypto from thousands of ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
item.description: OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence (AI) agent and take ov...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: $4.8M in crypto stolen after Korean tax agency exposes wallet seed
item.description: South Korea's National Tax Service accidentally exposed the mnemonic recovery phrase of a seized cryptocurrency wallet in an official press release, allowing hackers to steal 6.4 billion won ($4.8M) w...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
item.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the p...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access After API Enablement
item.description: New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints and access private da...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic Supply Chain Risk Over AI Military Dispute
item.description: Anthropic on Friday hit back after U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth directed the Pentagon to designate the artificial intelligence (AI) upstart as a "supply chain risk." "This action follows mon...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
item.description: HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
item.description: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
